steps.title: Find the textual signal
steps.content: The text says the front parlor "had always been her special province" — Tia Ana uses it for her school. Now the salon takes it over each evening, leaving it disordered for class the next morning. She comes "into the room several times to see if these guests had departed yet."

steps.title: Match the motivation
steps.content:
Tia Ana's repeated checking shows she's eager for the guests to leave so she can reset the room for her school. Choice C captures this: she wants the gathering to disperse so she can ready the space for her own needs. A misreads her motive (no evidence she finds guests uninteresting), B introduces "plans for a new school" not in the text, and D adds a different need elsewhere in the house.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
